summary: PR #94622 is the canonical maintainer-editable branch, but it is not merge-ready for automerge: the hydrated artifact shows ClawSweeper still requiring maintainer acceptance for the added build environment variable and exact-head CI has relevant failures, including core tooling and build-artifacts. Emit a bounded repair plan for the existing contributor branch; do not merge or close.

actions.reason: Branch is safe to update, but automerge cannot proceed until the repair loop handles failing relevant checks and gets a fresh review/maintainer acceptance for the new build env surface.
A complete executable repair artifact exists for the current contributor branch.

needs_human: Before merge, a maintainer still needs to accept the added build-only OPENCLAW_TSDOWN_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB environment variable unless a fresh ClawSweeper review clears that product/config-surface concern.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair the existing #94622 branch so the tsdown heap override remains narrow, uses the repo's strict positive-integer env parsing, passes focused build-wrapper tests, and clears the relevant changed checks before a fresh ClawSweeper review.
repair_candidate.pr_body:
What Problem This Solves

Constrained build hosts sometimes need to set the tsdown Node heap cap explicitly when automatic cgroup/proc memory detection is too high or too low.

Why This Change Was Made

This repairs the existing #94622 branch in place, preserving @tayoun's source contribution while keeping the override scoped to the tsdown build wrapper. The repair should keep existing cgroup/proc memory detection as the fallback, parse OPENCLAW_TSDOWN_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB with the existing strict positive-integer env parser, and avoid broader build/config churn.

User Impact

Operators running OpenClaw builds can opt into a build-only tsdown heap cap without changing unrelated NODE_OPTIONS behavior.

Evidence

Planned validation: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts; git diff --check; pnpm check:changed. ClawSweeper review and exact-head CI must be refreshed after repair.
credit_notes: Preserve original contributor credit on #94622: @tayoun authored the source PR and first commit.
Repair the existing PR branch rather than replacing it, so attribution stays in the original branch and PR history.
If release-note context is needed, keep it in the PR body or squash message; do not edit CHANGELOG.md for this normal PR.
